content:
We’ve been doing nothing but building flimsy API wrappers for two years to enable our applications to communicate with LLMs. But not anymore, thanks to Angular 22. Let me show you how we can integrate the AI agent into our application.

4 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Earlier this year, while finalizing a massive enterprise dashboard migration from Angular 18 to 21, the product team handed down a classic mandate: “We want an AI copilot. But it can’t just answer questions — it needs to be able to navigate the app, apply filters, and interact with the user’s current session.”
Historically, building this was an architectural nightmare.
You had to write custom backend wrappers. You had to serialize the user’s frontend state, send it to a server, have the server prompt the LLM, parse the JSON response, send it back via WebSockets, and then manually trigger Angular state changes. It was a fragile, high-latency mess of custom glue code.
With the release of Angular 22, the framework team has fundamentally solved this by natively adopting Anthropic’s open-source Model Context Protocol (MCP). Using the new provideExperimentalWebMcpTools API, you can…
